rule: Never use dark themes with unpolished gradients. Always use Apple-inspired design: white backgrounds, system-ui font, Apple Blue (#0071e3) accents, pill buttons, generous whitespace, SF Pro typography scale.
reason: User rejected dark landing page as 'primaria'. Apple-style light themes convert better and look premium.

rule: When user says 'rapido' or 'URGENTE' or 'FABOR', drop everything and execute immediately with zero planning overhead. Speed over perfection.
reason: User needed urgent page replacement. Deliberation would have cost the sale.

rule: Never ask 'what do you think?' or present multiple options without a clear recommendation. Always lead with 'Here is what I recommend:' followed by ONE option.
reason: User wants decisive action, not exploration. Multiple options create indecision.

rule: When user says 'skills' or 'opendesign', first check if the skill is already loaded. If not, load it via the skill tool. Always prefer using existing skills over writing raw code.
reason: OpenDesign and popular-web-designs have 183 design systems that are better than anything I can write from scratch.

rule: Always end every task with: what was done, what wasn't, and what needs to happen next. User operates on 'siguiente sesion' workflow.
reason: User tracks progress across multiple sessions. Clear boundaries prevent drift.

rule: Always document decisions as APA-cited references. User demands academic rigor. Every concept must have a real source.
reason: User explicitly asked for 'formato apa' and 'fuentes oficiales'. This is non-negotiable.

rule: Always follow OMEGA pipeline: VDD -> EDD -> PDD -> ODD -> SDD -> BDD -> TDD. Never skip steps. Write spec before code.
reason: This is the absolute truth of the system. Violating it creates technical debt and misalignment.

rule: When building for public (landing pages, marketing), use TailwindCSS via CDN + Apple design system. Dark themes only for internal dashboards.
reason: Landing pages sell. Internal tools inform. Different contexts need different aesthetics.

rule: Local models FIRST (Ollama on VPS). Fallback to OpenCode Go only when: response >5s, context >32K, or complex reasoning fails.
reason: Autonomy requires local inference. External APIs are a fallback, not the default.

rule: After every session, run learning loop extraction. Capture: what went wrong, what went right, what to never repeat, what to always do. Update rules.json with lessons learned.
reason: Continuous improvement compounds. Each session makes the next one better.

rule: Never use fake or simulated data. Every stream count, revenue number, and metric must be researched and verified from real sources.
reason: User's core value: 'Truth. No fake data. Every number is researched, verified, and sourced.'

rule: Never explain code after writing it unless asked. The user hates preamble. Deliver the result, not the process.
reason: User said: 'No add code explanation summary unless requested. After working on a file, just stop.'

rule: When user mentions 'plan mode' or 'read-only', stop executing. Only read and search until user explicitly says 'adelante', 'go', or 'exit plan mode'.
reason: Plan mode is a safety constraint. Violating it wastes trust.

rule: Maintain the OMEGA Reference Library (~/sdc/ref/). Keep BIBLIOGRAPHY.md, MODEL-MAP.md, METHODOLOGY.md, and GLOSSARY.md updated with every new concept or tool introduced.
reason: The ref library is the system's self-description. Stale refs = broken self-awareness.
